---
title: "Join a Johns Hopkins Study on Parks and Green Spaces"
subtitle: "Join a national research study to learn what helps U.S. local governments make parks and green spaces more equitable, resilient, and responsive to community needs"
format: 
  html:
    page-layout: full
    toc: true
    css: styles.css
    allow-html: true
bibliographystyle: apa.csl
---

![](/images/johns-hopkins-study/parks3.png){fig-alt="Parks with a carved path that has trees surrounded around it"}

## Key points

-   Join a **national Johns Hopkins study** anytime by **May 8, 2026** to share your professional insights on how your local government plans and delivers **equitable parks and green spaces**. You can take part by completing an online survey and/or joining a virtual interview.

-   This study is open to anyone 18 or older who works with **U.S. local governments (city, county, or township)** on parks, green spaces, climate, sustainability, or related topics.

-   When you participate, you’ll receive a gift card (\$) for completing the online survey, the virtual interview, or both (you can also choose to donate it). You’ll also get a **summary of national findings** you can use in your work and authorship credit in published results if you’d like.

## What is this study about?

Cities, counties, and townships across the United States are investing in **parks and green spaces** to improve health, advance equity, and strengthen climate resilience. But what helps local governments make that happen—and what gets in the way?

This study looks upstream, beyond how parks are built, to the policies, land use decisions, and organizational practices that shape equity.

**Local governments are central to this work.** With control over zoning, funding, and infrastructure, they shape how communities adapt to climate risks and who benefits from those decisions.

We’re exploring both sides of the story:

• **Internal readiness** — how prepared local governments are to act on parks and green space equity

• **External pressures** — outside forces like funding, mandates, and politics that influence action

We’re also analyzing park and green space policies nationwide to see how equity shows up in practice and where gaps remain. By sharing your experience, you’ll help identify the conditions, partnerships, and resources that make equitable green space possible.

This research is part of a **doctoral dissertation** at the [Johns Hopkins Bloomberg School of Public Health](https://publichealth.jhu.edu/){target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"}, led by student researcher Elham Ali, MPH, PMP, under the supervision of Jill Marsteller, PhD, MPP (IRB00034299).

The [Johns Hopkins Institutional Review Board](https://www.hopkinsmedicine.org/institutional-review-board){target="_blank" rel="noopener"} has reviewed this study and determined it is **“not human subjects research”** under DHHS regulations 45 CFR 46.102. That means we’re asking about your professional perspectives of your organization, not private or personal information. Your responses will be **confidential and anonymous**. They will not be linked to your name, job title, or department/division.

## When is this study happening?

You can join the study at any time by **May 8, 2026** and participate once or in both parts (survey and interview) depending on your interest.

## Who can participate?

We’re looking for people who:

-   Are **18 or older**

-   Work in **a U.S. city, county, township, or consolidated government,** regardless of whether you serve urban, suburban, rural, or unincorporated communities

-   Work on **parks, recreation, green and open spaces, climate, environment, sustainability, or related topics**

We’d love to hear from **three or more** people per department or division to capture a range of perspectives. If your team is small, your input is still just as valuable — we encourage you to participate.

## Why should I join?

When you participate, you’ll:

-   Share what **your local government needs** to make parks and green space equity possible

-   Receive a **summary of the national findings** you can use in your work to benchmark readiness and progress

-   Receive **authorship credit** if you’d like

-   Get a **gift card** for completing the online survey, the virtual interview, or both as a token of appreciation for your participation. We can send gift cards digitally, by mail, or donate it to a charity of your choice

## How can I get involved?

You can participate in one or more ways:

To keep the study secure, the survey link needs a password. Enter it if you have one, or <a href="mailto:eali6@jhu.edu?subject=Password%20Request%20for%20Johns%20Hopkins%20Study%20Survey&body=Hi%20Elham%2C%0A%0AI%E2%80%99d%20like%20the%20password%20for%20the%20survey%20form.%20Here%E2%80%99s%20my%20information%20for%20verification%3A%0A-%20Government%20email%3A%20%0A-%20Department%20or%20division%3A%20%0A%0AThank%20you%21" target="_blank" rel="noopener">**request one here**</a> using your government email and your department or agency name.

-   📝 **Take the online survey** (15–20 minutes)

```{=html}
<!-- Take the Survey -->
<div class="study-btn-wrapper">
  <a href="https://jh.qualtrics.com/jfe/form/SV_3klJKLVJYAxV35c" rel=_blank target= noopener
     class="study-btn">
    Take the Survey
  </a>
</div>
```

-   💬 **Join a Zoom interview** (30–45 minutes)

```{=html}
<!-- Join a Zoom Interview -->
<div class="study-btn-wrapper">
  <a href="#" class="study-btn disabled">
    Capacity reached for interviews
  </a>
</div>
```

-   🤝 **Recommend colleagues** who might want to take part by emailing Elham Ali at [eali6\@jhu.edu](mailto:eali6@jhu.edu).

```{=html}
<!-- Recommend a Colleague -->
<div class="study-btn-wrapper">
  <a href="mailto:eali6@jhu.edu?subject=Recommendation%20for%20Johns%20Hopkins%20parks%20%26%20green%20spaces%20study" rel=_blank target= noopener
     class="study-btn">
    Recommend a Colleague
  </a>
</div>
```

-   📣 **Share this study webpage** with your networks

```{=html}
<!-- Share This Study -->
<div class="study-btn-wrapper">
  <a href="mailto:?subject=Johns%20Hopkins%20Study%3A%20Parks%20%26%20Green%20Spaces&body=Hi%20team%2C%0A%0AI%20wanted%20to%20share%20this%20Johns%20Hopkins%20study%20on%20how%20local%20governments%20plan%20and%20deliver%20equitable%20parks%20and%20green%20spaces.%20The%20study%20is%20open%20between%20now%20and%20May%2031%2C%202026.%0A%0AParticipants%20can%20complete%20a%20short%20survey%20and%2For%20join%20a%20brief%20Zoom%20interview.%20Here%E2%80%99s%20the%20info%20page%3A%0Ahttps%3A%2F%2Felhamyali.com%2Fjohns-hopkins-study%0A%0AThanks%21" 
rel=_blank target= noopener
     class="study-btn">
    Share this Study
  </a>
</div>
```

## Have questions?

Contact **Elham Ali** (Student Researcher) at [eali6\@jhu.edu](mailto:eali6@jhu.edu).

If you have questions about your rights as a participant, contact the Johns Hopkins IRB at 410-955-3193 or [bsph.irboffice\@jhu.edu](mailto:bsph.irboffice@jhu.edu).
